https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fJHMbqUd: A suspense thriller set in the world of small town church politics, Dangerous Calling follows the story of Pastor Evan Burke and his wife Nora as they assume leadership of First Baptist Willit Springs after the mysterious death of the previous pastor. Eager to get to know the new pastor, Miss Pat, the church's controlling widow-woman, invites Evan and Nora to stay with her while she has the parsonage renovated. Left alone at Miss Pat's beautiful home in the mountains of North Georgia, Nora begins to suspect that Miss Pat's extremely sheltered adult son, Elijah, may have had something to do with the previous pastor's death. Although the story involves Christian characters, Dangerous Calling is not your typical church film. Owing more to Psycho and Misery than Left Behind, Dangerous Calling is sure to keep you on the edge of the pew. In this church the politics can be deadly.

I spent a few weeks building this Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles toy diorama for my bookshelf.  I love toys and I love miniatures.  I was looking for a way to display my Ninja Turtles - this was a perfect solution and so much fun to work on.  It's such a fun conversation piece for my home.  

Most of the skills I used in this project were learned from Black Magic Craft - check him out:  https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC2Rlv-ug-mtnXuMwlpcqFgg

Follow me on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jeremiahdaws/
And check out the SECRET behind my bookcase.  https://www.instagram.com/p/B5GZCvOJQZK/

**** UPDATE - AFTER OVER A YEAR OF STUDYING TO BE A MACHINIST, DON'T ATTEMPT THIS.  TOO MANY THINGS COULD GO WRONG!


Lately, I've been interested in machining and milling after watching Adam Savage on Tested have so much fun with it.  I decided to dip my toe in that water by adding a cross slide vise and some end mills to my drill press.  This is my first attempt at milling an actual useful part.  I have had no training other than watching some YouTube videos on the subject.  I may be doing this completely wrong but it's fun to try new things.

This video was shot and edited on the iPhone 11 Pro Max using FILMIC PRO and Luma Fusion.

Subscribe to my channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCAXM...

Follow me on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jeremiahdaws/

I made these simple shelves for frames.  They don't require many tools and are easy to build in a day.  Anyone can make them.  Many can be found for purchase online but making your own allows you to build to a custom size to fit your wall.  I created these two for some friends who couldn't find the right size.  This was a very easy project and I hope it inspires you to make something custom for your home as well.

This video was shot on the iPhone 11 Pro Max and edited on the iPad Pro using Luma Fusion.

This is the first in a series of eight Tormach Garage Series videos by our friend and fellow Machinist, Jeremiah Daws. He will be walking you, the new mill user, through: Powering Up Your Machine, the PathPilot Interface, Workholding, Toolholding and Tools, Running Coolant, CAD/CAM, and Conversational Programming.

Each of these topics will cover the basics of CNC to teach you the basic concept and to get you using your machine right away. This series of videos can also be used by our growing network of technical education teachers and makerspaces to introduces these milling basics to your CNC students. 

Subscribe to Jeremiah's YouTube page here: https://www.youtube.com/@JeremiahDaws/videos

https://vimeo.com/1115834134: Big Fish is a sweeping, heartfelt stage musical brought to life by the Artios Drama Department. It tells the story of Edward Bloom — a father whose larger-than-life tales blur the line between truth and imagination — and his son Will, who struggles to understand the man behind the stories. Set against a backdrop of wonder, humor, and deep emotion, the show invites us to wrestle with legacy, family, and the power of storytelling. At its heart, Big Fish celebrates the courage to dream big and love well, reminding us that the stories we tell and the love we give can outlive us and point to something greater and eternal.
https://vimeo.com/1077808429: Stand by Me is a moving dance film collaboration between the High School Choreo Club II and the High School Production Club, interpreting the timeless themes of the song through movement and story. Set inside a quiet theater, the piece follows one girl who begins alone, unsure of her place, until she’s slowly drawn into the rhythm and embraced by a community of dancers. With heartfelt choreography and cinematic visuals, the video captures a powerful message: we were never meant to stand alone—and when we show up for each other, beauty unfolds.
https://vimeo.com/1088826211: Moving Away is a heartfelt short film from our High School Production Club, written and directed by Kate Dantier. It follows the story of a girl who spends one last afternoon in imaginative play with her best friend—while carrying the secret that she’s moving away and hasn’t told her yet. Set against a backdrop of childhood wonder and emotional honesty, the film gently explores themes of courage, friendship, and the ache of letting go. At its core, Moving Away reminds us that even goodbyes can reflect a deeper truth: love leaves a lasting imprint, and the bonds we form often point us to something greater and eternal.
https://vimeo.com/1060658039: Experience the passion, mystery, and resilience of Jane Eyre: A Musical Drama, a stirring adaptation of Charlotte Brontë’s timeless novel. This powerful and emotionally rich production brings Jane’s journey to life through a hauntingly beautiful score, evocative staging, and a compelling ensemble cast.  With sweeping melodies, gothic intrigue, and a heroine who defies the odds, this production explores love, sacrifice, and the pursuit of true freedom. From the shadowed halls of Thornfield to Jane’s unwavering search for identity and belonging, Jane Eyre: A Musical Drama is a moving and unforgettable theatrical experience.
https://vimeo.com/1060278500: Embark on an unforgettable adventure with Peter and the Starcatcher! This high-energy, imaginative prequel to Peter Pan takes audiences on a journey filled with humor, heart, and limitless creativity.  With whimsical storytelling, inventive staging, and a dynamic ensemble cast, this production brings to life the origins of Neverland, Captain Hook, and the beloved boy who wouldn’t grow up. Featuring swashbuckling pirates, mysterious starstuff, and non-stop theatrical magic, Peter and the Starcatcher is a delight for audiences of all ages.
https://vimeo.com/960757455: Step into the enchanting world of Broadway’s “Beauty and the Beast,” a visually mesmerizing adaptation of the timeless fairy tale. With breathtaking sets, stunning costumes, and Alan Menken’s unforgettable music, including beloved songs like “Be Our Guest” and “Beauty and the Beast,” this production brings the magic of Belle and the Beast’s story to life. Audiences of all ages will be captivated by the romance, adventure, and charm of this classic Broadway spectacle.
https://vimeo.com/966837924: Our end-of-the-year dance and choir recital, Pages in Motion: The Power of Perspective, showcased the incredible talent of all our elementary, junior high, and high school choirs, along with diverse dance styles from our dance classes. The event brought stories to life through song and dance, highlighting the unique perspectives of each performer. From classical ballet to contemporary dance, and from harmonious choir performances to dynamic group numbers, this recital celebrated creativity, artistry, and the transformative power of performance, providing an enchanting evening that inspired and entertained audiences of all ages.
https://vimeo.com/892911174: Step into the spotlight with us as we unveil a captivating journey behind the scenes of Artios Academies of Sugar Hill's latest theatrical production—The Little Mermaid. This feature-length documentary is your exclusive pass to witness the passion, dedication, and camaraderie that transform long days and late nights into an enchanting spectacle.  From heartfelt moments to delightful shenanigans, experience the pulse of creativity, collaboration, and genuine connection as we showcase why this event is the pinnacle of the Artios experience.
https://vimeo.com/985143246: Check out this special inside look at the Artios Sugar Hill Dance Department, highlighting our 2024 "Pages In Motion" Dance Recital at the Gas South Theatre!
https://vimeo.com/966904877:
We're passionate about teaching our students to navigate secular environments while staying true to their faith and values. Through reflective discussions and open dialogue over the course of the competition weekend and beyond, we aim to promote personal and spiritual growth, encouraging exploration at the intersection of faith and the arts.

This year’s competition dance specially highlights the importance of community during difficult times. Despite showing that adversity is inevitable, the song offers a message of hope and support and allows us to reflect the importance of togetherness.
https://vimeo.com/894992137: Artios Academies of Sugar Hill High School choir presents "How Can I Keep From Singing" by Rev. Robert Lowry at the High School Festival at Bob Jones University. In this singular and impactful presentation, the choir's voices resonate with raw emotion, bringing to life the timeless lyrics that speak of resilience and unwavering faith. Against the backdrop of the esteemed festival venue, the choir's expression becomes a powerful testament to the enduring nature of hope in the face of life's challenges.
https://vimeo.com/895008821: Explore the world of creativity as the Artios Academies of Sugar Hill Art Department competes at Bob Jones University. Students showcased their talents across diverse mediums, from vibrant paintings to intricate sculptures. The competition highlighted the school's commitment to fostering artistic excellence and providing a platform for students to express themselves through various forms of art.
